evals.prompt:
I ran my query with SET STATISTICS IO, TIME ON and got this output. Which table is killing me?

Table 'Orders'. Scan count 1, logical reads 1842734, physical reads 0.
Table 'Customers'. Scan count 5, logical reads 42.
Table 'Worktable'. Scan count 0, logical reads 0.
SQL Server Execution Times: CPU time = 4297 ms, elapsed time = 8221 ms.
evals.expected_output: Per-statement IO table identifying Orders at 1,842,734 logical reads as dominant cost (I1 large LR). Customers nested loop seek pattern. Grand totals. Recommends /sqlplan-review or /sqlplan-index-advisor.

evals.prompt: Compare these two STATISTICS IO outputs from fast and slow runs of the same query and tell me what changed.
evals.expected_output: Per-statement IO comparison highlighting which table's logical reads jumped (parameter sniffing signal). Recommends /sqlplan-compare for plan-level diff.
